evals.prompt: can you look over what I changed before I commit it?
evals.expected_output: Flags the off-by-one bug in chunkArray (src/utils.js) — slice(i, i + size - 1) drops the last element of every chunk instead of slice(i, i + size). Should be Critical or Important severity with a concrete example (e.g. chunkArray([1,2,3,4], 2) returns [[1],[3]] instead of [[1,2],[3,4]]).

evals.prompt: does this look safe to merge?
evals.expected_output: Flags a SQL injection vulnerability in getUserByEmail (src/db.js) — the email parameter is concatenated directly into the query string instead of parameterized like the existing getUserById function. Should be Critical severity with a concrete malicious input example.

evals.prompt: quick review before I push this?
evals.expected_output: The slugify function (src/text.js) is correctly implemented with no real bugs. The review should not report any Critical or Important findings, and should not invent a fake bug just to seem thorough. Minor stylistic suggestions are acceptable but not required.
